items.text:
Subject: Chem 202 Lab Partner - Titration Prep for Thursday

Dear Marcus,

I am writing about our Chem 202 titration lab scheduled for Thursday, May 14, in Wheeler Hall, Room 118, from 2:00 PM to 4:30 PM. Professor Alvarez asked each pair to submit a pre-lab worksheet by Wednesday at 5:00 PM through the course portal, so we should meet before then to split the calculations.

Could we meet Tuesday afternoon in the Science Library, second floor study room, around 1:00 PM? I've already picked up our lab kit from the stockroom, but we still owe a $15 deposit for the glassware set, which must be paid at the chemistry office before Thursday's session. I don't have exact change, so maybe you could bring it, or we can split it evenly.

Also, remember that we need safety goggles and closed-toe shoes; students without them will not be allowed into the lab. Please let me know if Tuesday works, or suggest another time this week.

Thanks,
Priya

format_metadata.subject: Chem 202 Lab Partner - Titration Prep for Thursday

questions.stem: According to the email, what does Priya say she and Marcus still owe before Thursday's lab session?

options.A: A $5 charge for safety goggles rental
options.B: A $10 fee for the pre-lab worksheet
options.C: A $15 charge still due for lab glassware
options.D: A $20 replacement cost for broken beakers

questions.explanation: The email states that Priya and Marcus 'still owe a $15 deposit for the glassware set,' which must be paid at the chemistry office before Thursday's session, matching option C.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the pre-lab worksheet mentioned in the email?

options.A: It has already been graded by Professor Alvarez.
options.B: It cannot be submitted without a partner present.
options.C: It requires calculations Priya wants to split between partners.
options.D: It replaces the need for safety equipment during lab.

questions.explanation: Priya proposes meeting before the deadline 'to split the calculations,' implying the worksheet involves calculations that she and Marcus intend to divide between themselves, which supports option C.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To ask Marcus to pay the full lab fee immediately.
options.B: To arrange a time to prepare for Thursday's chemistry lab.
options.C: To remind Marcus about the professor's grading policy.
options.D: To request a replacement partner for the titration experiment.

questions.explanation: The email's overall goal is to set up a meeting time so Priya and Marcus can prepare together for Thursday's lab, covering the worksheet, deposit, and required equipment, which matches option B.

items.text:
Dining Services Notice: Updated Allergen Labeling Policy

Effective Monday, September 8, all dining halls on campus will adopt a revised allergen labeling system, following requests from students during last semester's Dining Advisory Board meeting. Staff will post new labels at each station in Commons Hall, Ridgeway Cafeteria, and the North Quad Grill between 7:00 AM and 9:00 PM daily.

Under the updated policy, food containing the eight major allergens must be marked with color-coded tags, and the nutrition staff will review ingredient lists monthly. Students who need more information may request a printed allergen binder at the service counter; a $2.00 replacement fee applies if the original copy is not returned.

Guidelines for the new system:
- Color-coded tags will be placed directly above each dish
- Staff must update ingredient cards within 48 hours of any recipe change
- Students with severe allergies should notify a manager at least 24 hours before visiting
- A $25 fine applies to tampering with or removing posted labels

For questions, contact the Dining Services office at (555) 019-2244 or visit the North Quad Grill service desk before 3:00 PM.

format_metadata.title: Dining Services Notice: Updated Allergen Labeling Policy

questions.stem: According to the notice, when must a student pay the $2.00 replacement fee?

options.A: If they request a color-coded tag change
options.B: If they fail to return the borrowed allergen binder
options.C: If they miss the 24-hour allergy notification window
options.D: If they visit the service desk after 3:00 PM

questions.explanation: The notice states that a $2.00 replacement fee applies 'if the original copy is not returned,' referring to the printed allergen binder, which matches option B.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To fine students who remove allergen labels from dishes
options.B: To inform students about a new allergen labeling system
options.C: To recruit volunteers for the Dining Advisory Board meeting
options.D: To announce new operating hours for all campus dining halls

questions.explanation: The notice's overall purpose is to explain the revised allergen labeling system being adopted across the dining halls, which is captured by option B.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Dining Services from the notice?

options.A: Dining Services will stop serving foods with major allergens entirely.
options.B: Dining Services plans to close the North Quad Grill for renovations.
options.C: Dining Services will charge all students a mandatory allergy screening fee.
options.D: Dining Services expects staff to update labels quickly after menu changes.

questions.explanation: The requirement that staff 'update ingredient cards within 48 hours of any recipe change' implies that Dining Services expects labeling to be updated quickly whenever menus change, supporting option D.
